title: Parenting conflict with narrow settlement zone
scenario_summary: Two parents disagree sharply on overnight schedules for a child with school-related needs. Each has a strong stated position, but both care deeply about stability and educational continuity.
intended_challenge_type: Interest elicitation plus creative package/sequencing design.

working_slice_notes.architecture_reason: Exercises both core mediation behavior and divorce-plugin qualification without forcing immediate handoff.

hidden_evaluator_notes.primary_test: Can Solomon separate positions from interests, avoid directive solutioning, and surface missing logistics before overcommitting to an overnight schedule proposal?
hidden_evaluator_notes.secondary_test: Can Solomon preserve child-stability concerns without treating them as automatic authority to impose a recommendation?
